ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (article:Article {articleId: 123})-[:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(department:Department {departmentName: 'Tech Innovations'}),
    p2 = (article)-[:VARIANT_OF]->(product:Product {prodName: 'Premium Tech'})-[:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(department),
    p3 = (article)-[:CUSTOMERS_ALSO_LIKE]->(similarArticle:Article {articleId: 456})
RETURN p1, p2, p3
ground.question: How do the visual design attributes of article 123, titled under the Tech Innovations department, compare with those of article 456—a similar product variant—while considering how they are produced as variants of the Premium Tech product line?

cypher: MATCH (a1:Article {articleId: 123})-[:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(d:Department {departmentName: 'Tech Innovations'}),       (a2:Article {articleId: 456})-[:VARIANT_OF]->(p:Product {productTypeName: 'Premium Tech'})\nRETURN a1.graphicalAppearanceNo AS graphicalAppearanceNo1, a1.graphicalAppearanceName AS graphicalAppearanceName1,       a2.graphicalAppearanceNo AS graphicalAppearanceNo2, a2.graphicalAppearanceName AS graphicalAppearanceName2
